author: Robert Carr
description: A hot turkey provolone sandwich with a seared turkey cutlet, garlic-Dijon mayo, mushroom-onion pan sauce, roasted red peppers, dressed arugula, and a toasted ciabatta or hoagie roll.
equipment: Cast-iron skillet or heavy saute pan

groceryIngredients.item: ciabatta roll or sturdy hoagie roll

ingredients: 1 boneless skinless turkey breast cutlet, about 6 ounces
3/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt or 1/2 teaspoon Morton kosher salt, divided
1/2 teaspoon black pepper, freshly ground if possible, divided
2 ounces fresh cremini mushrooms, sliced 1/4 inch thick
1 (12-ounce) jar roasted red peppers, using 2 tablespoons drained strips and 1/2 teaspoon pepper brine
1 ciabatta roll or sturdy hoagie roll, split
1/2 ounce baby arugula, about 1/2 packed cup
instructions: Move an oven rack 6 inches below the broiler and heat the broiler on high.
Line a rimmed baking sheet with aluminum foil.
Pat the turkey breast cutlet very dry with paper towels.
If the turkey cutlet is thicker than 1/2 inch, place it between sheets of parchment or plastic wrap and pound it to an even 1/2 inch thickness.
Season both sides of the turkey cutlet with 1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, 1/8 teaspoon black pepper, 1/4 teaspoon smoked paprika, 1/8 teaspoon garlic powder, and 1/4 teaspoon dried thyme.
Sprinkle 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour over a plate.
Press the seasoned turkey cutlet lightly into the flour on both sides, then shake off the excess flour so only a thin coating remains.

Make the garlic-Dijon mayo by stirring together 2 tablespoons mayonnaise, 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard, 1/2 garlic clove, finely grated, 1/4 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 teaspoon red wine vinegar, 1/2 teaspoon roasted red pepper brine, a tiny pinch Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and a pinch black pepper in a small bowl.
Refrigerate the garlic-Dijon mayo until assembly.
Heat a cast-iron skillet or heavy saute pan over medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon extra-virgin olive oil to the hot skillet.
Add the floured turkey cutlet.
Cook the turkey cutlet for 2 to 3 minutes on the first side, until golden brown.
Flip the turkey cutlet with tongs and cook the second side for 2 to 3 minutes, until golden brown and the thickest part reaches 160 degrees F.
Transfer the cooked turkey cutlet to a plate.
Loosely cover the cooked turkey cutlet with foil while making the pan sauce.
Reduce the skillet heat to medium and add 1/2 tablespoon unsalted butter.
Add the 2 ounces sliced cremini mushrooms, the thinly sliced yellow onion, a pinch Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and a pinch black pepper.
Cook the mushrooms and onion for 7 to 8 minutes, stirring every minute, until the mushrooms are browned, the onion is softened, and most of the moisture has cooked off.
Stir in 1 minced garlic clove and the remaining 1/4 teaspoon dried thyme.
Cook the garlic and thyme for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Add 2 tablespoons low-sodium chicken broth, the remaining 1/4 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce, and 1/2 teaspoon red wine vinegar.
Simmer the mushroom-onion pan sauce for 2 to 3 minutes, scraping the browned turkey bits from the skillet, until the liquid reduces by about half and coats the mushrooms and onions.
Return the turkey cutlet and any juices from the plate to the skillet.
Turn the turkey cutlet in the mushroom-onion pan sauce for 30 seconds so it is coated and hot.
Turn the heat to low so the turkey and mushroom-onion pan sauce stay warm while the roll toasts.
In a small bowl, stir together the remaining 1/2 tablespoon softened unsalted butter, the remaining 1/2 garlic clove, finely grated, and a tiny pinch Diamond Crystal kosher salt.
Place the split ciabatta roll or hoagie roll cut-side up on the foil-lined baking sheet.
Spread the garlic butter evenly over the cut sides of the split roll.
Broil the buttered roll for 1 to 2 minutes, watching constantly, until the cut sides are lightly toasted around the edges.
Move the toasted top roll to a plate and leave the toasted bottom roll cut-side up on the baking sheet.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic-Dijon mayo on the toasted bottom roll.
Place 1 slice provolone cheese on the mayo-coated bottom roll.
Place the hot turkey cutlet on the bottom roll, folding or trimming the turkey as needed so it fits the bread.
Spoon the mushroom-onion pan sauce over the turkey cutlet.
Add about 2 tablespoons drained roasted red pepper strips to the sandwich.
Tear the remaining 1 slice provolone cheese over the roasted red peppers.
Broil the open sandwich for 2 to 3 minutes, until the provolone is melted and lightly browned in spots.

While the cheese melts, toss 1/2 ounce baby arugula with the remaining 1/2 teaspoon red wine vinegar, the remaining 1 teaspoon extra-virgin olive oil, a tiny pinch Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and a pinch black pepper.
Top the hot sandwich with the dressed arugula.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic-Dijon mayo on the toasted top roll.
Close the sandwich and serve immediately while the turkey, mushroom-onion pan sauce, and provolone are hot.
notes: This sandwich is hot by temperature, not spice; the flavor comes from browned turkey, mushroom-onion pan sauce, roasted red peppers, garlic-Dijon mayo, toasted bread, and melted provolone.
Turkey breast cutlets cook quickly; for this version, one cutlet keeps the recipe compact.
Pounding any thick turkey pieces to 1/2 inch helps them cook evenly before the flour coating over-browns.
The thin flour coating gives the turkey better browning and helps the chicken broth reduce into a light pan sauce instead of tasting watery.
Use low-sodium chicken broth so the pan sauce can reduce without becoming too salty.
Roasted red pepper brine seasons the mayo with a little sweetness and acidity; use the liquid from the jar, not extra vinegar.
If cooking more than one turkey cutlet, do not crowd the skillet. Crowding traps steam and prevents browning.
The turkey is pulled at 160 degrees F because it will stay hot in the skillet and under the broiler during assembly.
Dress the arugula right before serving so it stays peppery and fresh against the hot turkey and cheese.
Serve immediately while the bread is crisp, the turkey is hot, and the provolone is melted.
title: Hot Turkey Provolone Sandwich
